across.clue: The control centre of a cell that contains DNA
across.hint: Remove this from a cell and it loses its 'instructions'

across.clue: Group of similar cells performing the same function
across.hint: Muscle cells group together to form this

across.clue: A tiny hair-like structure on some bacteria used for movement
across.hint: A bacterium spins this tail-like structure to swim

across.clue: The process of using microbes to produce beer or bread (yeast activity)
across.hint: Yeast converts sugar to alcohol and CO₂ through this process

across.clue: Membrane-bound structures within a cell (e.g. mitochondria)
across.hint: Mitochondria are the 'powerhouse' — a type of this

down.clue: Single-celled microorganism without a defined nucleus
down.hint: Some cause disease, others make your curd — these tiny cells are everywhere

down.clue: Organisms composed of many cells (e.g. humans)
down.hint: You are one — built from trillions of cells working together

down.clue: Microscopic plant-like organisms that perform photosynthesis in water
down.hint: These tiny green organisms float in oceans and produce oxygen

down.clue: Single-celled organism with a nucleus, e.g. Amoeba or Paramecium
down.hint: Amoeba uses pseudopodia; Paramecium uses cilia — both belong here

down.clue: Microbes used to enrich soil through nitrogen fixation
down.hint: Microbes that decompose farm waste produce this natural fertiliser
